title: rhabdophyllum calophyllum
intro: Rhabdophyllum calophyllum, also known as the "African stinkwood," is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Sapindaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which contain compounds with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Rhabdophyllum calophyllum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support skin health, making it a popular ingredient in traditional remedies. In herbal preparations, the leaves are often used in teas, infusions, and extracts to harness their medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm their effectiveness and safety.
